edge_cases.edge_case_description: The wording 'can directly support' and 'where implemented' makes 100 Kbps normal-mode support appear optional or configuration-dependent, creating a verification edge case over whether this mode must be present in all delivered implementations.

The phrase 'where correct hardware is included' makes 400 Kbps fast-mode support conditional on unspecified external or implementation-specific hardware, leaving verification unclear on when fast-mode behaviour is required to be tested.
The wording 'can support' means 400 Kbps fast-mode operation is not clearly mandatory for every implementation, creating an edge case over whether absence of fast-mode support is compliant.

The statement that reset level 'can be set to either active high or active low' implies a configurable reset polarity, but does not define whether both polarities must be supported in every implementation instance or how the selected polarity is fixed for verification.
